mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts signals a synchronized demand shock that overrides typical monetary easing benefits, causing equity valuations to reprice downward over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO decline × HOUST decline) fails to explain more than 10% of the variance in 60-day forward returns, or positive SPX returns occur in over 60% of triggered contraction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising policy rates increase borrowing costs and compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 50% of tightening episodes, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL is included in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real personal consumption expenditures and retail sales data confirms underlying economic momentum, prompting institutional investors to increase equity allocations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of instances, or the rolling 3-month Spearman correlation between the composite demand index and SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index declines by more than 1.5 percent month-over-month, supply chain disruptions or capacity utilization drops trigger earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward information coefficient remains below 0.02 over any 1-year window, or SPX 60-day returns are not statistically lower than the unconditional mean during triggered supply contractions.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in unemployment and fall in payroll employment indicates a deteriorating labor market that suppresses consumer demand, forcing equity multiples to contract and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample prediction, or positive SPX returns occur in over 55% of triggered deterioration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing market contraction regime, falling housing starts amplify wealth effects and reduce construction-related corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market sentiment shifts risk-off.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of housing contraction episodes, or the mechanism shows no incremental predictive power over a baseline FEDFUNDS model.
